<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is mentioning a checkbox for "accepting any parameters with the tool call" in the tool invocation test. I found the relevant section around lines 951-988. Let me look at the specific styling of the checkbox:

```jsx
<button
  onClick={() =>
    updateToolConfig(tool.id, {
      acceptAnyParameterValues:
        !tool.acceptAnyParameterValues,
    })
  }
  className={`w-5 h-5 rounded border flex-shrink-0 flex items-center justify-center transition-colors cursor-pointer ${
    tool.acceptAnyParameterValues
      ? "bg-foreground border-foreground"
      : "border-border hover:border-muted-foreground"
  }`}
>
  {tool.acceptAnyParameterValues && (
    <svg
      className="w-3 h-3 text-background"
      fill="none"
      viewBox="0 0 24 24"
      stroke="currentColor"
      strokeWidth={3}
    >
      <path
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
        d="M4.5 12.75l6 6 9-13.5"
      />
    </svg>
  )}
</button>
```

The issue is that when the checkbox is NOT checked:
- `border-border` - this is using the border color which might be too subtle in both light and dark modes
- There's no background color specified for the unchecked state, so it defaults to transparent

When checked:
- `bg-foreground border-foreground` - foreground color for background and border
- The checkmark icon uses `text-background` 

The problem is that when it's unchecked, the border uses `border-border` which might be too subtle, especially in light mode. The unchecked state needs a more visible border or background to make it clear it's a clickable checkbox.


I should improve the visibility of this checkbox by adding a background color for the unchecked state (like `bg-background`) and making the border more visible in both states. Let me check the globals.css to understand the color variables better.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the color variables:

Light mode:
- `--border: #e5e5e5` - very light gray, almost invisible on white background
- `--background: #ffffff` - white
- `--foreground: #1a1a1a` - dark gray/black

Dark mode:
- `--border: #262626` - dark gray, can be hard to see on dark backgrounds
- `--background: #0f0f0f` - very dark gray
- `--foreground: #ededed` - light gray/white

The issue is clear now. The checkbox when unchecked has:
- `border-border` - which is `#e5e5e5` in light mode and `#262626` in dark mode
- No background color specified (transparent)

This makes the checkbox nearly invisible especially in light mode where the light border blends into the light background, and similarly in dark mode.

To fix this, I should:
1. Add a background color to the unchecked state so it stands out
2. Make the border more visible by using a darker/more contrasting color

Let me update the checkbox styling to make it more visible:

```jsx
<button
  onClick={() =>
    updateToolConfig(tool.id, {
      acceptAnyParameterValues:
        !tool.acceptAnyParameterValues,
    })
  }
  className={`w-5 h-5 rounded border flex-shrink-0 flex items-center justify-center transition-colors cursor-pointer ${
    tool.acceptAnyParameterValues
      ? "bg-foreground border-foreground"
      : "bg-background border-muted-foreground hover:border-foreground"
  }`}
>
```

I'll change:
- Add `bg-background` for the unchecked state
- Change `border-border` to `border-muted-foreground` for better visibility
- Change hover from `hover:border-muted-foreground` to `hover:border-foreground` for clearer hover feedback</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Great, no lint errors. The changes I made:

1. Changed `border` to `border-2` - makes the border thicker (2px instead of 1px) so it's more visible
2. Changed unchecked border from `border-border` (very light gray) to `border-muted-foreground` (more visible gray)
3. Added `bg-background` to unchecked state to ensure solid background
4. Changed hover from `hover:border-muted-foreground` to `hover:border-foreground` for clearer hover feedback
5. Added `text-foreground` to the label text for consistency

These changes should make the checkbox clearly visible in both light and dark modes.</details></think>

---

I've fixed the checkbox visibility issue. The changes made:

1. **Thicker border**: Changed from `border` (1px) to `border-2` (2px) for better visibility
2. **More visible border color**: Changed unchecked state from `border-border` (very light `#e5e5e5` in light mode / `#262626` in dark mode) to `border-muted-foreground` (`#737373` in light mode / `#a3a3a3` in dark mode)
3. **Solid background**: Added `bg-background` to the unchecked state to ensure the checkbox has a clear background
4. **Better hover feedback**: Changed hover state to `hover:border-foreground` for clearer interaction feedback
5. **Consistent label color**: Added `text-foreground` to the label text

The checkbox should now be clearly visible in both light and dark modes.

```diff
      onSubmit(config);
    };
+ 
+   const handleBackdropClick = () =&gt; {
+     setShowCloseConfirmation(true);
+   };
+ 
+   const handleConfirmClose = () =&gt; {
+     setShowCloseConfirmation(false);
+     onClose();
+   };
+ 
+   const handleCancelClose = () =&gt; {
+     setShowCloseConfirmation(false);
+   };
  
    if (!isOpen) return null;
```

```diff
        {/* Backdrop */}

+

+         &lt;div className="fixed inset-0 z-[60] flex items-center justify-center"&gt;

+             className="absolute inset-0 bg-black/30"

+           &lt;div className="relative bg-background rounded-xl shadow-2xl border border-border p-6 max-w-sm w-full mx-4"&gt;

+               Discard changes?
+             &lt;/h3&gt;
+             &lt;p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-6"&gt;
+               You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to close this dialog? Your changes will be lost.
+             &lt;/p&gt;
+             &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-end gap-3"&gt;
+               &lt;button
+                 onClick={handleCancelClose}
+                 className="h-10 px-4 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium bg-background text-foreground hover:bg-muted transition-colors cursor-pointer border border-border"
+               &gt;
+                 Cancel
+               &lt;/button&gt;

+                 onClick={handleConfirmClose}

+               &gt;
```

Great, no lint errors. The changes I made:

---

Done! I've added a confirmation dialog that appears when clicking outside the Add Test dialog. The changes include:

1. **New state**: `showCloseConfirmation` to track if the confirmation should be shown
2. **Backdrop click handler**: Instead of closing directly, clicking outside now shows the confirmation dialog
3. **Confirmation dialog UI** with:
   - Title: "Discard changes?"
   - Message explaining that changes will be lost
   - **Cancel** button - stays on the dialog
   - **Discard** button (red) - closes the dialog

The confirmation dialog appears above everything else (z-index 60) and clicking outside the confirmation dialog cancels it (keeps you on the Add Test dialog).

```diff
     - Used for: Tools, Personas, Scenarios creation/editing
  3. **Modal Dialogs**: Centered overlays for confirmations and simple forms
-    - Backdrop click closes dialog
```

```diff
  - **Never use hardcoded colors** like &#96;bg-black&#96;, &#96;bg-[#1a1a1a]&#96;, &#96;text-white&#96;, &#96;border-[#333]&#96; - these break light mode
  - **Always use CSS variable classes**: &#96;bg-background&#96;, &#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;border-border&#96;, &#96;bg-muted&#96;, &#96;bg-popover&#96;
+ - **Checkboxes need visible borders**: Use &#96;border-muted-foreground&#96; (not &#96;border-border&#96;) and &#96;border-2&#96; for custom checkbox buttons to ensure visibility in both light and dark modes
  
  ### Forms
```

---

Updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with:

1. **Modal Dialogs section** - Distinguished between simple dialogs (backdrop closes directly) and form dialogs with unsaved data (shows confirmation before closing)

2. **Styling gotchas** - Added guidance on checkbox visibility: use `border-muted-foreground` and `border-2` for custom checkbox buttons

3. **State Management gotchas** - Added the unsaved changes confirmation pattern for form dialogs

4. **New Custom Checkbox code example** - Added a complete reusable pattern for button-based checkboxes that work in both light and dark modes

---
